paper_map.title: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
paper_map.abstract_summary: This study investigates the co-precipitation behavior and mechanism of nanoscale Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel. Cu addition promotes κ-carbide precipitation, and κ-carbide facilitates the formation of FCC Cu-rich phase, both coherent with the austenite matrix in a cube-on-cube orientation relationship. Lattice misfit calculations show Cu-rich phase has a lower nucleation barrier in the austenite, leading to Cu-rich precipitation adjacent to κ-carbide particles. The austenite lattice parameter is identified as a critical index for κ-carbide precipitation.
paper_map.research_objective: To clarify the co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel.
main_process_variables: austenite matrix lattice parameter (critical value ~0.3670 nm)
expected_information_axis: elemental partitioning (EDS mapping of Fe, Mn, Al, Cu)
